hero.headline: Structured Product Labeling Made Simple
hero.subheadline: A 5-project .NET 8.0 platform for FDA drug label management with 40+ API endpoints, AI-powered natural language queries via Claude, and Model Context Protocol integration.

stats.label: API Endpoints
stats.description: Search, CRUD, import, AI interpret, and navigation views

stats.label: Integrated Projects
stats.description: Static site, REST API, MCP server, console importer, shared library

stats.label: AI Skills
stats.description: Indication discovery, dose conversion, pharmacologic class matching, and more

features.title: SPL Parsing & Normalization
features.description: Import and process pharmaceutical labeling data from DailyMed ZIP files. Automatic parsing according to FDA SPL specifications with complete HL7 compliance.

features.title: Intelligent Data Analysis
features.description: Comprehensive navigation views for products, ingredients, packaging hierarchies, and labeler information. Search and filter across all label sections.

features.title: AI-Powered Interface
features.description: Natural language interaction with your pharmaceutical data. Ask questions like 'Find all products containing aspirin' and get structured responses.

features.title: Enterprise Security
features.description: Encrypted identifiers, OAuth authentication with Microsoft and Google, role-based access control, and comprehensive activity logging.

features.title: High Performance
features.description: Optimized batch processing, intelligent caching, and efficient data structures designed for Azure SQL and scalable cloud deployment.

features.title: Import & Export
features.description: Bulk import from DailyMed data sources with progress tracking. Generate valid SPL XML output from database records for regulatory submission.

featureShowcase.title: AI-Powered Drug Intelligence
featureShowcase.description: Ask natural language questions like 'Find drugs indicated for hypertension' and get structured, sourced responses using the request-interpret-execute-synthesize pattern.
featureShowcase.detail: Claude interprets your queries, maps them to the right API endpoints, executes them, and synthesizes the results into clear, actionable answers with full source attribution.
featureShowcase.imageAlt: MedRecPro AI indication search showing structured drug results

featureShowcase.title: MCP Protocol for Claude.ai
featureShowcase.description: Connect Claude desktop or Claude.ai directly to your pharmaceutical data through the Model Context Protocol with full OAuth 2.1 security.
featureShowcase.detail: Dynamic client registration, PKCE S256, automatic user provisioning, and JWT-based authentication. Claude discovers tools via RFC 9728/8414 metadata and calls them with structured parameters.
featureShowcase.imageAlt: MedRecPro MCP tool response showing drug class data in Claude

featureShowcase.title: Complete Label Rendering
featureShowcase.description: 18 RazorLight templates render SPL XML into structured HTML covering every section of an FDA drug label including ingredients, packaging, and clinical data.
featureShowcase.detail: Full HL7 compliance with the FDA SPL Implementation Guide (Dec 2023). Import labels from DailyMed ZIP files, normalize the data, and render human-readable documents from the database.
featureShowcase.imageAlt: MedRecPro rendered SPL drug label document

howItWorks.title: How MedRecPro Works
howItWorks.subtitle: A modern approach to pharmaceutical labeling management

steps.title: Import SPL Data
steps.description: Upload ZIP files containing SPL XML documents from DailyMed. MedRecPro parses and normalizes the data.

steps.title: Explore & Analyze
steps.description: Navigate through products, ingredients, organizations, and packaging hierarchies using the REST API or AI-powered natural language queries.

steps.title: Manage & Update
steps.description: Full CRUD operations for all label sections. Track changes, compare versions, and maintain compliance with regulatory requirements.

steps.title: Export & Submit
steps.description: Generate valid SPL XML files from your database records.

useCases.title: Built for Pharmaceutical Professionals
useCases.subtitle: Explore what you can accomplish with MedRecPro

cases.title: Search Drug Products
cases.description: Search by NDC code, ingredient, labeler, application number, or pharmacologic class across normalized FDA data

cases.title: Analyze Formulations
cases.description: Explore active moieties, UNII codes, ingredient relationships, and strength breakdowns across product hierarchies

cases.title: Track Packaging
cases.description: Navigate complex packaging hierarchies with NDC codes, marketing categories, and equivalence codes

cases.title: Orange Book Data
cases.description: Access FDA Orange Book patent, exclusivity, and therapeutic equivalence data across 7 normalized tables

cases.title: Query with AI
cases.description: Natural language queries interpreted by Claude using the request-interpret-execute-synthesize pattern

cases.title: Generate Labels
cases.description: Render SPL XML from database records using 18 RazorLight templates for regulatory review and submission

api.title: Comprehensive REST API
api.subtitle: Full programmatic access to all functionality

endpoints.description: Retrieve paginated records for any label section
Import SPL data from ZIP files with progress tracking
Map natural language queries to API endpoint calls
Synthesize API results into human-readable responses
Search indications across all drug labels
Generate valid SPL XML from database records
Advanced ingredient search with UNII and moiety filters

cta.title: Ready to Simplify Your SPL Workflow?
cta.subtitle: Sign in with your Microsoft or Google account to access the full API

introduction.content: Thank you for using MedRecPro! Please read these Terms of Service ('Terms') carefully before accessing or using MedRecPro. These Terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you and MedRecPro governing your access to and use of our pharmaceutical labeling management platform and related services.

summary.description: For your convenience, we have presented a brief summary of key sections. This summary is not legally binding; please read the full Terms below.

items.description: Key terms used throughout this agreement with specific meanings.
Requirements for creating and maintaining an account on MedRecPro.
Rules you must follow when using MedRecPro.

items.section: D. User Content and SPL Data
items.description: Your rights and responsibilities regarding content you upload.
Open source licensing and intellectual property rights.
Terms governing use of AI analysis features.
Your responsibility for FDA and other regulatory compliance.
Terms for free and paid service tiers.
How to cancel your account and what happens to your data.
The service is provided 'as is' without warranties.
Limits on our liability for damages.
How we will notify you of changes to these Terms.
These Terms are governed by U.S. federal law and the laws of Maryland.

sections.heading: A. Definitions
sections.content: The following terms have specific meanings throughout this agreement. Understanding these definitions will help you interpret these Terms correctly.

subsections.content: These are the foundational terms used throughout this agreement:

items.description: Your registered user account on MedRecPro, which represents your legal relationship with us and provides access to the Service.
Refers collectively to these Terms of Service, our Privacy Policy, and all other operating rules, policies, and procedures published on MedRecPro.
All materials available through the Service, including text, data, articles, images, software, SPL documents, and other materials, whether provided by MedRecPro or Users.
Refers to MedRecPro, the pharmaceutical labeling management platform, and its operators.
The applications, software, products, and services provided by MedRecPro, including the web application, API, and any related tools or features.
The individual, company, or organization that has registered for an Account, accesses the Service, or uses any part of the Service.
Content that you create, upload, or submit to the Service, including SPL documents, annotations, notes, and other materials.

subsections.content: Terms specific to pharmaceutical labeling:

items.description: The FDA's standard for submitting product labeling content in XML format, as defined by HL7 specifications.
Health Level Seven International, the organization that develops standards for healthcare data exchange, including SPL specifications.
The United States Food and Drug Administration, the regulatory body responsible for pharmaceutical product labeling requirements.
FDA regulations establishing criteria for electronic records and electronic signatures.

sections.heading: B. Account Terms
sections.content: To use certain features of MedRecPro, you must create an Account. This section describes your responsibilities regarding your Account.

subsections.content: You must register for an Account to access most features of the Service. Registration requires:

items.description: You must authenticate using a supported provider (Microsoft, Google, or Apple). We do not store passwords directly.
You must provide accurate and complete information during registration and keep your account information current.
MedRecPro is designed for professional use by pharmaceutical, healthcare, and regulatory professionals.

subsections.content: The following requirements apply to all Accounts:

items.description: You must be at least 18 years old to create an Account. MedRecPro is a professional tool not intended for minors.
Each individual may maintain only one personal Account. Organizations may have multiple user accounts under an organization subscription.
Accounts must be registered by humans. Automated account creation is not permitted without prior written authorization.
You may not use MedRecPro in violation of applicable laws, including export control or sanctions laws of the United States.

subsections.content: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your Account:

items.description: You are responsible for all activity that occurs under your Account, including actions taken by others with access to your Account.
You must notify us immediately at support@medrecpro.com if you become aware of any unauthorized access to or use of your Account.
Your Account security depends in part on your authentication provider. Ensure your Microsoft, Google, or Apple account is properly secured.

subsections.content: If you access MedRecPro through an organization account provided by your employer or institution, additional terms may apply as specified in your organization's agreement with us. Your organization's administrator may have control over your account and access to your data.

sections.heading: C. Acceptable Use
sections.content: Your use of MedRecPro must comply with this Acceptable Use Policy. Violation of these policies may result in termination of your Account.

subsections.content: You agree to use the Service only for lawful purposes and in compliance with:

items.description: All applicable federal, state, local, and international laws and regulations.
FDA regulations regarding pharmaceutical labeling, including 21 CFR Part 201 and related requirements.
U.S. export control and sanctions laws. You may not use the Service if you are subject to U.S. sanctions or export restrictions.

subsections.content: You may not engage in the following activities:

items.description: Attempting to access accounts, systems, or data without authorization, or bypassing security measures.
Interfering with or disrupting the Service, servers, or networks connected to the Service.
Uploading or transmitting viruses, malware, or other malicious code.
Using automated means to scrape, harvest, or extract data from the Service without authorization.
Impersonating another person or entity, or falsely claiming affiliation with any person or entity.
Using the Service to create or process fraudulent or intentionally misleading pharmaceutical labeling documents.

subsections.subheading: C.3 Rate Limits and Fair Use
subsections.content: To ensure service availability for all users, we may implement rate limits on API requests and certain features. Excessive or abusive use that impacts service performance for other users is prohibited.

sections.heading: D. User Content and SPL Data
sections.content: You retain ownership of content you upload to MedRecPro. This section describes your rights and responsibilities regarding User Content.

subsections.content: You retain all ownership rights to User Content you upload, create, or submit to the Service. MedRecPro does not claim ownership of your SPL documents, annotations, or other User Content.
By uploading User Content to the Service, you grant MedRecPro a limited license to:

items.description: Store, process, display, and transmit your User Content as necessary to provide the Service.
Parse, validate, render, and transform SPL documents according to FDA and HL7 specifications.
Create backup copies of your User Content for data protection and disaster recovery purposes.

subsections.content: You are solely responsible for:

items.description: The accuracy, completeness, and regulatory compliance of all User Content, including SPL documents.
Ensuring you have all necessary rights, licenses, and permissions to upload and use the User Content.
Reviewing and verifying all content before submission to regulatory authorities. MedRecPro is a tool to assist your workflow, not a substitute for professional judgment.
Appropriately handling any confidential, proprietary, or trade secret information in your User Content.

subsections.content: We reserve the right to remove User Content that violates these Terms, applicable law, or poses security risks. We will attempt to notify you before removing content except where prohibited by law or in emergency situations.

MedRecPro is not designed to store or process Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined by HIPAA. Do not upload patient-identifiable health information to the Service. If you inadvertently upload PHI, notify us immediately at privacy@medrecpro.com.

sections.heading: E. Intellectual Property and Open Source
sections.content: MedRecPro is an open-source application. This section describes the applicable licenses and intellectual property rights.

subsections.content: The MedRecPro software is provided under the Apache License, Version 2.0. You may obtain a copy of the License at https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0. Subject to the terms of the Apache 2.0 License, you are free to use, modify, and distribute the software.
The source code for MedRecPro is available on GitHub. Contributions to the project are welcome under the terms of the Apache 2.0 License and our Contributor License Agreement.

The MedRecPro name, logo, and other branding are trademarks of MedRecPro. The Apache 2.0 License does not grant rights to use these trademarks. You may not use our trademarks in a way that suggests endorsement or affiliation without prior written permission.
MedRecPro incorporates third-party open-source components, each subject to its own license terms. A list of third-party components and their licenses is available in the project repository.

If you provide feedback, suggestions, or ideas about the Service ('Feedback'), you grant MedRecPro a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable license to use, modify, and incorporate the Feedback into the Service without obligation to you.

sections.heading: F. AI-Powered Features
sections.content: MedRecPro offers AI-powered analysis features. This section describes the terms governing use of these features.

subsections.content: AI-powered features are provided through third-party AI services, including Anthropic's Claude API. Your use of AI features is subject to both these Terms and the applicable third-party terms of service.

subsections.subheading: F.2 Data Processing for AI Features
subsections.content: When you use AI-powered features, relevant portions of your queries and document content may be sent to AI service providers for processing. We do not use your content to train AI models. See our Privacy Policy for details on data processing.
AI-generated analysis and suggestions are provided for informational purposes only:

items.description: AI outputs do not constitute regulatory, legal, medical, or professional advice.
You must independently verify all AI-generated content before relying on it for regulatory submissions or business decisions.
We make no warranties regarding the accuracy, completeness, or suitability of AI-generated content.

subsections.content: AI features may be modified, limited, or discontinued at any time. Availability may vary based on your subscription tier and usage limits.

sections.heading: G. Regulatory Compliance
sections.content: MedRecPro is designed to support pharmaceutical labeling workflows. This section clarifies compliance responsibilities.

subsections.content: You are solely responsible for ensuring your use of MedRecPro complies with all applicable regulatory requirements, including:

items.description: FDA regulations for pharmaceutical labeling, including 21 CFR Part 201, SPL submission requirements, and related guidance documents.
If your use of MedRecPro is subject to 21 CFR Part 11 requirements for electronic records and signatures, you are responsible for implementing appropriate controls.
Applicable pharmaceutical labeling regulations in other jurisdictions where your products are marketed.
HL7 SPL Implementation Guide specifications and other applicable industry standards.

subsections.content: MedRecPro is a tool to assist pharmaceutical labeling workflows. It is not a validated system under 21 CFR Part 11 and does not replace your organization's quality management systems, validation procedures, or regulatory compliance programs.

MedRecPro does not provide regulatory advice. Information displayed in the Service regarding FDA requirements or SPL specifications is for reference only. Consult with qualified regulatory professionals for compliance guidance.

MedRecPro maintains activity logs for security and operational purposes. If you require specific audit trail capabilities for regulatory compliance, you are responsible for verifying that MedRecPro's logging meets your requirements.

sections.heading: H. Payment
sections.content: MedRecPro offers both free and paid service tiers. This section describes payment terms for paid features.

subsections.content: MedRecPro provides a free tier with basic functionality. Free tier features and limitations are described on our website and may change over time.
Paid subscription tiers provide access to additional features, higher usage limits, and priority support. Current pricing is available at https://medrecpro.com/pricing.
For paid subscriptions:

items.description: Subscriptions are billed in advance on a monthly or annual basis as selected during signup.
We accept payment via credit card and other methods as displayed during checkout.
Subscription prices may change. We will provide at least 30 days' notice of price increases, and changes will apply at your next billing cycle.
Prices do not include applicable taxes. You are responsible for all taxes associated with your subscription.

subsections.content: Subscription fees are generally non-refundable. We may, at our discretion, provide refunds or credits in cases of billing errors or service issues.

sections.heading: I. Cancellation and Termination
sections.content: You may cancel your Account at any time. This section describes cancellation procedures and data retention.

subsections.content: You may cancel your Account at any time through your account settings or by contacting support@medrecpro.com. Upon cancellation, your right to access the Service terminates immediately.
Upon Account cancellation:

items.description: You may export your User Content before cancellation. We recommend downloading all data you wish to retain.
We will delete your User Content within 90 days of cancellation, except where retention is required for legal obligations.
Deleted content may persist in encrypted backups for up to 30 additional days.

subsections.content: We may suspend or terminate your Account if you violate these Terms, engage in prohibited activities, or for other reasons with notice. In cases of severe violations, we may terminate immediately without notice.

Upon termination, your right to use the Service ceases. Provisions of these Terms that by their nature should survive termination will survive, including ownership provisions, warranty disclaimers, and limitations of liability.

sections.heading: J. Disclaimer of Warranties
sections.content: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED 'AS IS' AND 'AS AVAILABLE' WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND.

subsections.content: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, MEDRECPRO DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION:

items.description: Any warranty of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose.
Any warranty that the Service will be accurate, reliable, error-free, or uninterrupted.
Any warranty that the Service will meet your regulatory compliance requirements.
Any warranty that the Service will be secure or free from vulnerabilities.
Any warranty regarding third-party services integrated with MedRecPro.

subsections.content: As open-source software provided under the Apache 2.0 License, MedRecPro is provided WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied, including without limitation any warranties or conditions of TITLE, NON-INFRINGEMENT, MERCHANTABILITY, or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

MedRecPro is a tool to assist your work. It does not replace professional judgment, regulatory expertise, or compliance programs. You assume full responsibility for decisions made based on Service outputs.

sections.heading: K. Limitation of Liability
sections.content: This section limits our liability for damages arising from your use of the Service.

subsections.content: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL MEDRECPRO BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION:

items.description: Loss of profits, revenue, business opportunities, or goodwill.
Loss of data, including User Content or SPL documents.
Costs arising from service interruption or inability to use the Service.
Claims by third parties arising from your use of the Service.
Regulatory enforcement actions, fines, or penalties related to your use of the Service.

subsections.content: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, MEDRECPRO'S TOTAL LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THESE TERMS OR YOUR USE OF THE SERVICE SHALL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF (A) THE AMOUNTS PAID BY YOU TO MEDRECPRO IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM, OR (B) ONE HUNDRED DOLLARS ($100).

The limitations and exclusions in this section apply regardless of the theory of liability and even if MedRecPro has been advised of the possibility of such damages. These limitations are an essential basis of the bargain between you and MedRecPro.
Some jurisdictions do not allow the exclusion of certain warranties or limitation of liability. In such jurisdictions, our liability shall be limited to the maximum extent permitted by law.

sections.heading: L. Indemnification
sections.content: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless MedRecPro and its officers, directors, employees, agents, and affiliates from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or related to:

subsections.content: Your indemnification obligation covers:

items.description: Your violation of these Terms or any applicable law or regulation.
Your User Content, including any claims that your content infringes third-party rights.
Your failure to comply with applicable regulatory requirements in your use of the Service.
Disputes between you and third parties related to your use of the Service.

subsections.content: We will provide reasonable cooperation in the defense of any claim and you will not settle any claim without our prior written consent if the settlement would impose obligations on us or admit liability on our behalf.

sections.heading: M. Communications
sections.content: This section describes how we will communicate with you regarding the Service.

subsections.content: You consent to receive communications from us electronically via the email address associated with your Account or through the Service. You agree that electronic communications satisfy any legal requirement for written communications.
We may send you notifications about your Account, service changes, security alerts, and other matters related to the Service. You may not opt out of critical service and security notifications.
MedRecPro provides support via email and in-Service communications. We do not currently offer telephone support. Contact us at support@medrecpro.com for assistance.

sections.heading: N. Changes to These Terms
sections.content: We may modify these Terms from time to time. This section describes how we will notify you of changes.

subsections.content: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. We will update the 'Last Updated' date at the top of these Terms when we make changes.

For material changes, we will provide at least 30 days' notice before the changes take effect. Notice may be provided via email to your registered email address or through a prominent notice on the Service.

Your continued use of the Service after the effective date of revised Terms constitutes your acceptance of the changes. If you do not agree to the revised Terms, you must stop using the Service and cancel your Account.
Previous versions of these Terms may be available upon request for reference purposes.

sections.heading: O. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
sections.content: This section describes the law that governs these Terms and how disputes will be resolved.

subsections.content: These Terms and any dispute arising out of or related to these Terms or the Service shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the United States of America and the State of Maryland, without regard to conflict of law principles.

You agree to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in the State of Maryland for any dispute arising out of or related to these Terms or the Service. You waive any objection to venue in such courts.

Before initiating any formal dispute resolution, you agree to first contact us at legal@medrecpro.com to attempt to resolve the dispute informally. We will attempt to resolve disputes within 30 days of receiving notice.

TO THE EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, YOU AND MEDRECPRO AGREE THAT EACH MAY BRING CLAIMS AGAINST THE OTHER ONLY IN YOUR OR OUR INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY AND NOT AS A PLAINTIFF OR CLASS MEMBER IN ANY PURPORTED CLASS OR REPRESENTATIVE ACTION.

sections.heading: P. Miscellaneous
sections.content: This section contains additional legal provisions.

subsections.content: These Terms, together with the Privacy Policy and any other agreements expressly incorporated by reference, constitute the entire agreement between you and MedRecPro regarding the Service and supersede all prior agreements and understandings.

If any provision of these Terms is held to be invalid or unenforceable, that provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable, or if modification is not possible, severed from these Terms. The remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.
Our failure to enforce any provision of these Terms shall not constitute a waiver of that provision or our right to enforce it in the future.

You may not assign or transfer these Terms or your rights under these Terms without our prior written consent. We may assign these Terms without restriction in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets.

MedRecPro shall not be liable for any failure or delay in performance due to causes beyond our reasonable control, including natural disasters, war, terrorism, strikes, government actions, or internet service provider failures.
Section headings are for convenience only and do not affect the interpretation of these Terms.

Provisions that by their nature should survive termination shall survive, including without limitation: ownership provisions, warranty disclaimers, indemnification, limitations of liability, and dispute resolution provisions.

sections.heading: Q. Contact Information
sections.content: If you have questions about these Terms, please contact us:

introduction.content: Welcome to the MedRecPro Privacy Policy. This document describes how MedRecPro ('we', 'us', or 'our') handles your Personal Data when you interact with our pharmaceutical labeling management platform and related services (collectively, 'Services'). MedRecPro is committed to protecting your privacy and ensuring the security of your information in compliance with applicable data protection laws.

sections.heading: 1. Data Controller
sections.content: MedRecPro operates as the Data Controller for Personal Data processed through our Services. For questions about data processing or to exercise your privacy rights, contact our Data Protection Officer at privacy@medrecpro.com.

subsections.content: If your employer, healthcare organization, or pharmaceutical company provides your MedRecPro account, they assume the role of Data Controller for most Personal Data used in our Services. In such cases, MedRecPro functions as a Data Processor, adhering to the Data Controller's instructions. Please refer to your organization's privacy policies for additional information.
When we provide training, consulting, or implementation services, the data protection terms in your service agreement govern the processing of your data.

sections.heading: 2. Personal Data We Collect
sections.content: The Personal Data we collect depends on how you interact with our Services, including which features you use and how you access them.

subsections.content: We collect information you provide directly, including:

items.description: When you create an account, we collect your name, email address, and profile information provided by your authentication provider (Microsoft, Google, or Apple).
Optional information you provide such as professional title, organization name, pharmacy license numbers, or other professional credentials.
Pharmaceutical labeling documents, SPL files, annotations, notes, and other content you upload or create within the Services.
Information you provide when contacting customer support, including descriptions of issues, screenshots, and communication history.
Information you submit through surveys, feature requests, or interactive feedback mechanisms.
For paid subscriptions, billing details including name, billing address, and payment method information processed through our payment provider.

subsections.content: When you use our Services, we automatically collect certain information:

items.description: Data about your interactions with the Services, including IP address, device information, browser type, session details, date and time of requests, pages viewed, features used, and search queries.
Operating system, device type, device identifiers, screen resolution, and application version.
Approximate geographic location based on IP address to provide region-appropriate services and comply with jurisdictional requirements.
Server logs that record your access to the Services, including timestamps, referring URLs, and actions performed.
Information about service performance, error reports, and diagnostic data to maintain and improve the Services.

subsections.content: We may receive information about you from third-party sources:

items.description: When you authenticate using Microsoft, Google, or Apple, we receive your name, email address, and profile information as permitted by your settings with those services.
We may collect publicly available professional information such as pharmacy license verification data from state boards of pharmacy.
If you are part of an organization account, administrators may provide information about you for account management purposes.

sections.heading: 3. How We Use Your Information
sections.content: We process your Personal Data for the following purposes:

subsections.content: To provide, maintain, and improve our pharmaceutical labeling management Services, including processing SPL documents, managing labeling workflows, and delivering personalized experiences.
To authenticate your identity, authorize access to features based on your role and permissions, maintain session security, detect and prevent fraud, abuse, and security threats.
To send service-related notifications, respond to support requests, provide updates about features or policy changes, and deliver information you have requested.
To analyze usage patterns, identify accessibility gaps, troubleshoot issues, and improve the Services' functionality, performance, and user experience.

When you use AI-powered features, your queries and relevant document content may be processed by third-party AI services (such as Anthropic's Claude) to generate responses and analysis. We do not use your content to train AI models.
To comply with applicable laws, regulations, and legal processes, including FDA regulations, healthcare data requirements, and responding to lawful requests from authorities.
To conduct billing, accounting, capacity planning, and other business operations necessary to provide the Services.

sections.heading: 4. SPL Data Processing
sections.content: MedRecPro is designed to process pharmaceutical labeling data in compliance with FDA and HL7 specifications.

subsections.content: You retain full ownership of all SPL documents, labeling content, and related data you upload or create through the Services. MedRecPro processes this data solely to provide the Services you request.

We process SPL data according to FDA Structured Product Labeling specifications and HL7 standards. Our processing supports, but does not replace, your organization's regulatory compliance obligations.
While we strive for accuracy in rendering and processing SPL data, you remain responsible for verifying the accuracy of pharmaceutical labeling content before submission to regulatory authorities.
MedRecPro is not designed to process Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined by HIPAA. Do not upload patient-identifiable health information to the Services.

sections.heading: 5. Sharing of Personal Data
sections.content: We may share your Personal Data with the following categories of recipients:

subsections.content: We engage trusted third-party service providers to perform functions on our behalf, including cloud hosting (Microsoft Azure), content delivery (Cloudflare), authentication services, payment processing, and analytics. These providers are contractually obligated to protect your data and use it only for the services they provide to us.
When you use AI-powered features, relevant data is processed by Anthropic's Claude API. Anthropic's data processing is governed by their privacy policy and our data processing agreement with them.

If you use MedRecPro through an organization account, your organization's administrators may have access to your account information and usage data as permitted by your organization's agreement with us.

We may disclose Personal Data when we believe in good faith that disclosure is necessary to comply with applicable laws or legal processes, protect the rights, property, or safety of MedRecPro, our users, or the public, or detect, prevent, or address fraud, security, or technical issues.

In the event of a merger, acquisition, reorganization, or sale of assets, your Personal Data may be transferred as part of that transaction. We will notify you of any such change and the choices you may have regarding your data.
We may share your Personal Data with other parties when you explicitly consent to such sharing.

sections.heading: 6. Data Storage and Security
sections.content: We implement comprehensive security measures to protect your Personal Data.

subsections.content: Your data is stored securely on Microsoft Azure infrastructure in the East US 2 region. Azure maintains SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and other security certifications.
We implement encryption at rest using AES-256 encryption for stored data and encryption in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher for all data transmission.

We employ role-based access controls, secure authentication mechanisms, and the principle of least privilege to limit access to Personal Data to authorized personnel who require it for legitimate business purposes.
We conduct regular security assessments, maintain security logs and monitoring, implement secure software development practices, and promptly address identified vulnerabilities.

We maintain incident response procedures to detect, respond to, and recover from security incidents. In the event of a data breach affecting your Personal Data, we will notify you and relevant authorities as required by applicable law.

sections.heading: 7. Data Retention
sections.content: We retain your Personal Data for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this Privacy Policy.

subsections.content: We retain your account information for as long as your account remains active. Upon account deletion, we will delete or anonymize your Personal Data within 90 days, except where retention is required for legal obligations or legitimate business purposes.
User-uploaded documents and content are retained until you delete them or request account deletion. Deleted content may be retained in backups for up to 30 days.
Service usage logs and analytics data are typically retained for up to 24 months for service improvement and security purposes, then deleted or anonymized.
We may retain certain data for longer periods when required by law, to resolve disputes, enforce our agreements, or protect our legal rights.

sections.heading: 8. Your Privacy Rights
sections.content: Depending on your location, you may have specific legal rights regarding your Personal Data.

subsections.content: Subject to applicable law, you have the right to:

items.description: Request access to the Personal Data we hold about you.
Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete Personal Data.
Request deletion of your Personal Data, subject to certain exceptions.
Receive your Personal Data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format.
Request restriction of processing of your Personal Data under certain circumstances.
Object to processing of your Personal Data for certain purposes.
Where processing is based on consent, withdraw that consent at any time.

subsections.content: To exercise your privacy rights, contact us at privacy@medrecpro.com. We will respond to your request within the timeframe required by applicable law (typically 30-45 days). We may need to verify your identity before processing your request.
You can access, update, and manage much of your account information directly through your account settings in the Services.
If we deny your rights request, you have the right to appeal that decision. We will provide you with information on how to submit an appeal at that time.

sections.heading: 9. Lawful Bases for Processing (EEA, UK, and Switzerland)
sections.content: If you are located in the European Economic Area (EEA), United Kingdom, or Switzerland, we process your Personal Data based on the following lawful bases under the GDPR:

subsections.content: Processing necessary to perform our contract with you, including providing the Services, managing your account, and delivering features you request.

Processing necessary for our legitimate interests, including improving the Services, ensuring security, conducting analytics, and communicating with you, provided these interests are not overridden by your data protection rights.
Processing necessary to comply with applicable laws and regulations.
Where required by law, we will obtain your consent before processing your Personal Data. You may withdraw consent at any time.

sections.heading: 10. International Data Transfers
sections.content: MedRecPro is based in the United States, and your Personal Data may be transferred to and processed in the United States and other countries where our service providers operate.

subsections.content: When we transfer Personal Data from the EEA, UK, or Switzerland to countries not recognized as providing adequate data protection, we rely on appropriate safeguards such as Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission.
We are committed to complying with applicable data transfer requirements and protecting your Personal Data regardless of where it is processed.

sections.heading: 11. Cookies and Tracking Technologies
sections.content: We use cookies and similar technologies to provide and improve our Services.

subsections.content: We use essential cookies for authentication, session management, security, and core functionality. These cookies are necessary for the Services to function and cannot be disabled.

We may use analytics cookies to understand how users interact with the Services, identify usage patterns, and improve functionality. You can opt out of analytics cookies through your browser settings.
We do not use advertising or third-party tracking cookies. We do not sell your Personal Data or share it for targeted advertising purposes.
You can manage cookie preferences through your browser settings. Note that disabling essential cookies may affect the functionality of the Services.
We honor Do Not Track (DNT) signals sent by your browser. When we detect a DNT signal, we will not set non-essential cookies.

sections.heading: 12. Third-Party Services
sections.content: Our Services integrate with or link to third-party services. This Privacy Policy does not apply to third-party services, and we encourage you to review their privacy policies.

subsections.content: We use Microsoft, Google, and Apple for authentication. Their privacy policies govern their collection and use of your data: Microsoft Privacy Statement (https://privacy.microsoft.com), Google Privacy Policy (https://policies.google.com/privacy), Apple Privacy Policy (https://www.apple.com/legal/privacy/).
Our Services are hosted on Microsoft Azure. Microsoft's privacy practices are governed by the Microsoft Privacy Statement and applicable data processing agreements.
AI-powered features are provided through Anthropic's Claude API. Anthropic's privacy policy (https://www.anthropic.com/privacy) governs their processing of data.
We use Cloudflare for DNS management and content delivery. Cloudflare's privacy policy (https://www.cloudflare.com/privacypolicy/) governs their data processing.

sections.heading: 13. US State-Specific Information
sections.content: This section provides additional information for residents of US states with specific data privacy laws.

subsections.content: If you are a California resident, you have additional rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA):

items.description: You have the right to request information about the categories and specific pieces of Personal Data we have collected about you.
You have the right to request deletion of your Personal Data, subject to certain exceptions.
You have the right to request correction of inaccurate Personal Data.
We do not sell your Personal Data or share it for cross-context behavioral advertising.
We will not discriminate against you for exercising your privacy rights.

subsections.content: Residents of Virginia, Colorado, and Connecticut have similar rights to access, correct, delete, and port their Personal Data, as well as the right to opt out of targeted advertising, sale of Personal Data, and profiling. You also have the right to appeal our decision regarding a rights request.

In the past 12 months, we have collected the following categories of Personal Information: identifiers (name, email address, IP address), professional information (job title, organization), internet activity information (usage data, log data), and geolocation data (approximate location based on IP address).

To exercise your rights under state privacy laws, email privacy@medrecpro.com or submit a request through your account settings. You may also designate an authorized agent to make requests on your behalf.

sections.heading: 14. Children's Privacy
sections.content: MedRecPro is designed for professional use and is not intended for individuals under the age of 18. We do not knowingly collect Personal Data from minors. If you become aware that a minor has provided us with Personal Data, please contact us at privacy@medrecpro.com, and we will take steps to delete such information.

sections.heading: 15. Changes to This Privacy Policy
sections.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, technologies, legal requirements, or other factors. If we make material changes, we will provide notice through the Services or by other means (such as email) at least 30 days before the changes take effect. Your continued use of the Services after the effective date of the updated Privacy Policy constitutes your acceptance of the changes.

sections.heading: 16. Contact Us
sections.content: If you have questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us:

subprocessors.description: The following third-party subprocessors may process Personal Data on our behalf:

list.name: Microsoft Azure
list.purpose: Cloud infrastructure, hosting, and data storage

list.name: Cloudflare
list.purpose: DNS management, content delivery, and security

mcpDocs.title: MedRecPro MCP Server
mcpDocs.description: This MCP (Model Context Protocol) server provides secure access to MedRecPro medical records functionality for AI assistants like Claude. It acts as an OAuth 2.1 gateway between MCP clients and the MedRecPro Web API.
mcpDocs.architectureDescription: The MCP server runs as an IIS virtual application at /mcp on the same Azure App Service as the main MedRecPro UI and API. IIS handles path-based routing to three separate ASP.NET Core processes:
mcpDocs.architectureDiagram:
Azure App Service: "MedRecPro" (Windows, IIS)
                        +--------------------------------------------------+
                        |                                                  |
+------------------+    |  /          site\wwwroot       MedRecProStatic   |
|  Claude / MCP    | HTTPS  /api       site\wwwroot\api   MedRecPro API    |
|  Client          |------->  /mcp       site\wwwroot\mcp   MedRecProMCP   |
+------------------+    |                                                  |
        |               +--------------------------------------------------+
        v                                    |
+------------------+                         v
|  Cloudflare      |                +------------------+
|  (CDN/WAF/DNS)   |                |  Azure Key Vault |
+------------------+                |  (medrecprovault)|
                                    +------------------+
mcpDocs.authDescription: This server implements OAuth 2.1 with PKCE. When Claude connects, it automatically handles the authentication flow:
authSteps: Server returns 401 with WWW-Authenticate header pointing to Protected Resource Metadata
Claude registers via /oauth/register (Dynamic Client Registration, RFC 7591)
User authenticates with Google or Microsoft
MCP server issues authorization code, redirects to Claude
Claude exchanges code for tokens via /oauth/token
Claude sends authenticated POST to /mcp with Bearer token
MCP server forwards token to MedRecPro API for each tool call
mcpDocs.oauthScopes: openid, profile, email, mcp:tools, mcp:read, mcp:write
mcpDocs.endpointsDescription: All endpoints are externally prefixed with /mcp by the IIS virtual application.
mcpDocs.endpointsNote: GET /mcp returns HTTP 405 (Method Not Allowed). This is correct behavior — the MCP Streamable HTTP transport only accepts POST requests.
mcpDocs.toolsDescription: Once authenticated, Claude automatically discovers the following tools when connecting to the /mcp endpoint. Tools are organized into two categories:

toolCategories.description: Search and retrieve FDA drug label information from the MedRecPro database. All drug data is sourced from official FDA Structured Product Labeling (SPL) documents.
toolCategories.workflowDiagram:
search_drug_labels ──► Quick lookups, section queries, comparisons
                       ├── ProductName, ActiveIngredient, UNII
                       ├── Label sections (Indications, Warnings, etc.)
                       └── ViewLabelUrl - link to FDA label

export_drug_label_markdown ──► Complete label export (TWO-STEP)
  Step 1: Search products ─────► User selects DocumentGUID
  Step 2: Export markdown ──────► FullMarkdown + ViewLabelUrl

search_expiring_patents ──► Orange Book patent searches
                            ├── Patent expiration dates
                            └── Generic availability timeline

search_by_pharmacologic_class ──► Drug class discovery
                                  ├── AI-powered class matching
                                  └── Products grouped by class

search_by_indication ──► Condition-based drug discovery
                         ├── AI-powered indication matching
                         └── Products grouped by indication

tools.name: search_drug_labels
tools.description: Searches for FDA drug labels and returns complete product details with markdown-formatted label sections.
tools.usageDescription: Use when the user asks specific questions about a drug (e.g., "What are the side effects of Lipitor?" or "Compare aspirin and ibuprofen").
tools.whenToUse: Questions about side effects, dosage, warnings, indications, drug interactions

parameters.name: productNameSearch
parameters.description: Brand/product name search (e.g., "Lipitor", "Advil"). Supports partial matching.

parameters.name: activeIngredientSearch
parameters.description: Generic/active ingredient name search (e.g., "atorvastatin", "ibuprofen"). Supports partial matching.

parameters.name: unii
parameters.description: UNII (Unique Ingredient Identifier) code for exact chemical match (e.g., "R16CO5Y76E" for aspirin).

parameters.name: sectionCode
parameters.description: LOINC section code to filter results to a specific label section. See section codes table below.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Results per page (1–50). Default: 10. Recommended: 3 for initial searches since labels are large.

tools.returnsDescription: Array of product objects containing ProductName, ActiveIngredient, UNII, DocumentGUID, markdown-formatted label Sections, and a ViewLabelUrl link to view the FDA label in a browser.

tools.name: export_drug_label_markdown
tools.description: Exports a complete FDA drug label as clean, well-formatted markdown.
tools.usageDescription: Use when the user wants a complete label (e.g., "Show me the full label for Lipitor" or "Export the label for atorvastatin").
tools.whenToUse: "Show me the full label for X" or "Export the label"

steps.label: Step 1 — Product Search
steps.description: Call without documentGuid to search for products.

parameters.name: unii
parameters.description: UNII code for exact ingredient match.

steps.returnsDescription: List of matching products with ProductName, ActiveIngredient, UNII, and DocumentGUID. The user selects the correct product.

steps.label: Step 2 — Export Markdown
steps.description: Call with documentGuid from Step 1 selection.
steps.returnsDescription: Complete label as formatted markdown with source links (ViewLabelUrl).

tools.returnsDescription: Complete FDA drug label as formatted markdown with source links.

tools.name: search_expiring_patents
tools.description: Searches Orange Book NDA patents by expiration date, brand name, or active ingredient.
tools.usageDescription: Use when the user asks about patent expiration or generic drug availability (e.g., "When will generic Ozempic be available?" or "What patents expire in 6 months?").
tools.whenToUse: Patent expiration dates, generic drug availability

parameters.name: tradeName
parameters.description: Brand name search (e.g., "Ozempic", "Lipitor"). Supports partial matching.

parameters.name: expiringInMonths
parameters.description: Patent expiration window in months (e.g., 6 for next 6 months). Must be > 0.

tools.returnsDescription: Patents with trade name, active ingredient, expiry date, patent number, and dosage form. Includes pre-rendered markdown table and links to FDA labels when available.

tools.name: search_by_pharmacologic_class
tools.description: Discovers groups of drugs by therapeutic or pharmacologic class using AI-powered natural language search.
tools.usageDescription: Use when the user asks about drug classes or groups (e.g., "Show me all beta blockers" or "What SSRIs are available?").

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: AI-powered natural language search (e.g., "beta blockers", "SSRIs", "statins"). Maps common terms to pharmacologic classes.

parameters.name: classNameSearch
parameters.description: Direct partial match on exact class names (fallback if AI search returns no results).

parameters.name: pageNumber
parameters.description: Page number (1-based, used with classNameSearch). Default: 1.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Results per page (1–200, used with classNameSearch). Default: 25.

tools.returnsDescription: Matched classes, products grouped by class with label links, total product count, and suggested follow-up queries.

tools.name: search_by_indication
tools.description: Discovers drugs that treat a specific medical condition or disease using AI-powered matching.
tools.usageDescription: Use when the user asks what drugs treat a condition (e.g., "What drugs treat high blood pressure?" or "What medications are used for diabetes?").

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Natural language condition search (e.g., "high blood pressure", "diabetes", "depression"). AI maps common terms to medical conditions.

tools.returnsDescription: Matched indications, products grouped by indication with label links, total product count, validation reason, and suggested follow-up queries.

toolCategories.description: Manage user profile and activity information. All operations require authentication.

tools.name: get_my_profile
tools.description: Gets the current authenticated user's profile information including name, email, and roles.
tools.whenToUse: "Who am I logged in as?" or "Show my profile"

tools.name: get_my_activity
tools.description: Gets the current user's recent activity log showing actions performed in the system.
tools.whenToUse: "What have I done recently?" or "Show my activity"

tools.name: get_my_activity_by_date_range
tools.description: Gets the current user's activity within a specific date range.
tools.whenToUse: "What did I do last week?" or "Show activity from January"

loincCodes.typicalQuestion: What is this drug used for?
When should I NOT use this?
Does this interact with other drugs?
Does this have a black box warning?

mcpDocs.loincNote: Not all FDA labels have every LOINC section code. If a query with a specific sectionCode returns empty results, retry without the sectionCode parameter to retrieve all available sections, then search the content for the relevant information.

toolSelectionGuide.userIntent: What are the side effects of X?
What is the dosage for X?
What are the warnings for X?
Show me the full label for X
When will generic X be available?
What drugs treat high blood pressure?
What medications are used for diabetes?
What did I do last week?

mcpSetup.title: MedRecPro MCP Server
mcpSetup.heroText: Connect Claude to the FDA's drug label database. Ask about any FDA-approved medication and get accurate, sourced answers from official labeling data.
mcpSetup.capabilitiesDescription: MedRecPro connects Claude to the FDA's Structured Product Labeling (SPL) database, giving you instant access to comprehensive drug label information. Ask Claude about any FDA-approved medication — side effects, dosage, warnings, drug interactions, indications — and get accurate, sourced answers pulled directly from official FDA labeling data. You can also search Orange Book patent expiration dates to discover when generic versions of brand-name drugs may become available.

features.title: Search by Brand or Generic Name
features.description: Ask about "Lipitor" or "atorvastatin" and get the same comprehensive results from official FDA labels.

features.title: Targeted Section Lookups
features.description: Get specific label sections like warnings, side effects, dosage, or drug interactions without wading through the full label.

features.title: Drug Comparisons
features.description: Compare warnings, side effects, or indications across multiple medications side by side.

features.title: Complete Label Export
features.description: Export any FDA drug label as clean, formatted markdown with all sections and source links.

features.title: FDA Source Verification
features.description: Every response includes a direct link to the official FDA label so you can verify the information.

features.title: Activity Tracking
features.description: View your search history and activity within MedRecPro to keep track of your research.

features.title: Patent & Generic Availability
features.description: Search Orange Book patent expiration dates to find out when generic versions of brand-name drugs may become available.

features.title: Drug Class Discovery
features.description: Search by pharmacologic class to discover all drugs in a therapeutic category like SSRIs, beta blockers, or statins.

features.title: Condition-Based Search
features.description: Ask what drugs treat a condition like high blood pressure, diabetes, or depression and get FDA-sourced results.

gettingStartedSteps.text: Visit the Claude Connectors Directory and find MedRecPro.
Click Connect to begin the setup process.
Authenticate with your Google or Microsoft account. No special permissions or account types are required.
Start asking Claude about medications. Try: "What are the side effects of Lipitor?"

mcpSetup.authInfo: OAuth 2.1 with PKCE — MedRecPro uses industry-standard OAuth 2.1 for secure authentication. Sign in with your existing Google or Microsoft account. No special permissions, subscriptions, or account types are required. Authentication is handled seamlessly through Claude's connector interface.

examples.title: Look Up Side Effects and Warnings
examples.prompt: What are the warnings and or adverse effects for atorvastatin
examples.description: Claude searches the FDA drug label database for atorvastatin, retrieves the warnings and adverse reactions sections, and presents a comprehensive summary including myopathy risks, immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy, liver enzyme considerations, and other safety information — all sourced directly from the official FDA label.
examples.screenshotAlt: Screenshot showing Claude retrieving warnings and adverse effects for atorvastatin from FDA label data
examples.caption: Claude retrieves and summarizes atorvastatin warnings and adverse reactions from the FDA label

examples.title: Check Drug Indications
examples.prompt: What does the carvedilol label say about its indications
examples.description: Claude queries the FDA label for carvedilol and returns its three approved indications: heart failure, left ventricular dysfunction following myocardial infarction, and hypertension. Each indication includes details about the approved use, with links to view the full FDA label for both tablet and capsule formulations.
examples.screenshotAlt: Screenshot showing Claude retrieving carvedilol indications from FDA label data
examples.caption: Claude presents carvedilol's three FDA-approved indications with full details

examples.title: Find Dosage Information
examples.prompt: What is the starting dose for gabapentin
examples.description: Claude searches the dosage and administration section of the gabapentin label, returning starting doses for each approved indication (postherpetic neuralgia and epilepsy), including titration schedules, pediatric dosing, renal impairment adjustments, and tapering guidance.
examples.screenshotAlt: Screenshot showing Claude retrieving gabapentin dosage information from FDA label data
examples.caption: Claude provides detailed gabapentin dosing by indication from the FDA label

examples.title: Export a Complete Drug Label
examples.prompt: Show me the full label for valsartan
examples.description: Claude first searches for all valsartan products, presenting options including single-ingredient generics, brand names (Diovan), and combination products. After you select a product, Claude exports the complete FDA label as formatted markdown with all sections — indications, dosage, warnings, adverse reactions, and more.
examples.screenshotAlt: Screenshot showing Claude exporting the full Diovan valsartan label as formatted markdown
examples.caption: Claude presents product options, then exports the complete Diovan label with all sections

examples.title: Search Patent Expiration Dates
examples.prompt: What drug patents are expiring in the next month
examples.description: Claude searches the FDA Orange Book for patents expiring within the next month, returning a consolidated table of upcoming expirations organized by product. The results include trade names, active ingredients, expiry dates, patent numbers, and dosage forms.
examples.screenshotAlt: Screenshot showing Claude searching for drug patents expiring in the next month from the FDA Orange Book
examples.caption: Claude presents upcoming patent expirations from the FDA Orange Book

examples.title: Discover Drug Classes
examples.prompt: Show me all the SSRIs available
examples.description: Claude uses AI-powered search to find the Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitor pharmacologic class, then returns all products in that class grouped by active ingredient with links to their FDA labels.
examples.screenshotAlt: Screenshot showing Claude discovering SSRI drug class members from MedRecPro
examples.caption: Claude discovers and lists all SSRIs with their FDA label links

examples.title: Check Your Authentication Status
examples.prompt: Using the MedRecPro connection tell me about my authentication status
examples.description: Claude retrieves your MedRecPro profile, showing your login name, email, account creation date, last login, last activity, timezone, and locale settings. This confirms your connection is active and working.
examples.screenshotAlt: Screenshot showing Claude retrieving user profile information from MedRecPro
examples.caption: Claude confirms your MedRecPro connection by showing your profile information

tools.name: search_drug_labels
tools.description: Search and retrieve specific label sections
tools.whenToUse: Questions about side effects, dosage, warnings, indications, drug interactions

tools.name: export_drug_label_markdown
tools.description: Export a complete label as formatted markdown
tools.whenToUse: "Show me the full label for X" or "Export the label"

tools.name: search_expiring_patents
tools.description: Search Orange Book patent expiration dates
tools.whenToUse: "When will generic Ozempic be available?" or "What patents expire in 6 months?"

tools.name: search_by_pharmacologic_class
tools.description: Discover drugs by therapeutic or pharmacologic class
tools.whenToUse: "Show me all beta blockers" or "What SSRIs are available?"

tools.name: search_by_indication

tools.whenToUse: "What drugs treat high blood pressure?" or "For diabetes?"

tools.name: get_my_profile
tools.whenToUse: "Who am I logged in as?" or "Show my profile"

tools.name: get_my_activity
tools.whenToUse: "What have I done recently?" or "Show my activity"

tools.description: View activity within a date range
tools.whenToUse: "What did I do last week?" or "Show activity from January"

mcpSetup.disclaimer: All drug data is sourced from official Structured Product Labeling (SPL) documents. MedRecPro does not provide medical advice — it provides access to the same labeling information available on the NIH National Library of Medicine's DailyMed website.
